mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rate compresses equity discount rates and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following low FEDFUNDS readings do not significantly exceed zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above eight percent signals insufficient aggregate demand and weak corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad demand slowdown that reduces expected corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of INDPRO and RSAFS does not produce a Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below fifty basis points, accelerating CPI inflation signals overheating that raises expectations of future monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, rising CPIAUCSL does not coincide with negative 20-day forward returns in > 60% of subsequent months.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects rising capacity utilization and earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to associate with positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in housing starts exceeding five percent signals weakening residential investment and construction activity, reducing aggregate demand expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines of greater than 5% over 3 months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and a falling unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that boosts wage income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of joint-signal months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth indicates a contraction in aggregate demand that compresses corporate profit expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.
